program.input: x, y, z, t, u, v = sp.symbols("x y z t u v")

subsection.title: Line integral of a 1-form
subsection.p:
Then
\int_C\omega = \int_a^b \left[ P(\mathbf r(t))x'(t) + Q(\mathbf r(t))y'(t) + R(\mathbf r(t))z'(t) \right]\,dt.

program.input:
x, y, z, t = sp.symbols("x y z t")
P = ... Q = ... R = ...
xt = ... yt = ... zt = ...
integrand = ( P.subs({x: xt, y: yt, z: zt})*sp.diff(xt, t) + Q.subs({x: xt, y: yt, z: zt})*sp.diff(yt, t) + R.subs({x: xt, y: yt, z: zt})*sp.diff(zt, t) )
value = sp.integrate(integrand, (t, a, b))

subsection.title: Line integral of a vector field
subsection.p: For \mathbf F=(P,Q,R), \int_C\mathbf F\cdot d\mathbf r uses the same formula as the 1-form P\,dx+Q\,dy+R\,dz.

subsection.title: Surface flux integral
subsection.p:
Then
\iint_S\mathbf F\cdot\widehat{\mathbf n}\,dS = \iint_D \mathbf F(\mathbf r(u,v)) \cdot (\mathbf r_u\times\mathbf r_v) \,du\,dv.

subsection.title: SymPy surface flux pattern

program.input:
x, y, z, u, v = sp.symbols("x y z u v")
P = ... Q = ... R = ...
xu = ... yu = ... zu = ...
r = sp.Matrix([xu, yu, zu]) ru = r.diff(u) rv = r.diff(v)
normal = ru.cross(rv)
F = sp.Matrix([ P.subs({x: xu, y: yu, z: zu}), Q.subs({x: xu, y: yu, z: zu}), R.subs({x: xu, y: yu, z: zu}) ])
integrand = sp.simplify(F.dot(normal))
value = sp.integrate( integrand, (u, u0, u1), (v, v0, v1) )

subsection.p: The order of (u,v) bounds must match the expression.

program.input: area_integrand = sp.sqrt(normal.dot(normal)) area = sp.integrate(area_integrand, (u, u0, u1), (v, v0, v1))
curlF = sp.Matrix([ sp.diff(R, y) - sp.diff(Q, z), sp.diff(P, z) - sp.diff(R, x), sp.diff(Q, x) - sp.diff(P, y) ])
divF = sp.diff(P, x) + sp.diff(Q, y) + sp.diff(R, z)
gradf = sp.Matrix([ sp.diff(f, x), sp.diff(f, y), sp.diff(f, z) ])
F = sp.Matrix([F1, F2, F3]) J = F.jacobian([x, y, z])

subsection.title: Orientation warnings
subsection.p: For a curve, reversing the parametrization changes the sign of a 1-form integral.
For a surface, switching u and v changes \mathbf r_u\times\mathbf r_v to -\mathbf r_u\times\mathbf r_v.
Flux changes sign when orientation reverses.

subsection.title: Symbolic warnings
subsection.p: A symbolic answer may be correct but hard to read.
Always check assumptions, especially signs and parameter ranges.
